paper_title: Separation and δ15N analysis of soil N species

methods.method_name: Separation and δ15N Analysis of Soil N Species
methods.description: This method describes the process of preparing soil samples for the analysis of nitrogen species, including dissolved inorganic nitrogen (DIN) and dissolved organic nitrogen (DON), and determining their δ15N values.

steps.description: The frozen FHAB and pedon samples were lyophilized, sifted, ground and homogenized.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare soil samples by lyophilizing, sifting, grinding, and homogenizing.
outputs: lyophilized, sifted, ground, and homogenized soil samples

steps.description: The F, H, A and B samples from the four directions were combined into one for a given tree, and used for the characterization of dissolved inorganic N (DIN; NH4 + and NO3 -) and dissolved organic N (DON).
steps.searchable_summary: Combine soil samples for characterization of DIN and DON.
outputs: combined soil sample for DIN and DON characterization

steps.description: The N species were extracted from the soil samples using water at a pH representative of rain, centrifuged and filtered.
steps.searchable_summary: Extract N species using water, centrifuge, and filter.

steps.description: The NH4 + and NO3- concentrations were determined using an ion chromatograph (930 Compact IC Flex, Metrohm).
steps.searchable_summary: Determine NH4+ and NO3- concentrations using ion chromatography.

steps.description: The NH4 was then separated using the diffusion method and trapped as (NH4)2SO4 on glass fibre filters.
steps.searchable_summary: Separate NH4 using diffusion and trap as (NH4)2SO4.

steps.description: The (NH4)2SO4-containing filters were dried in desiccator and analysed using an elemental analysis -continuous flow -isotope ratio mass spectrometry (EA-CF-IRMS) system.
The NO3 was extracted following the chemical method including Cd for reducing nitrate into nitrite, and sodium azide to produce nitrous oxide (N2O).
steps.searchable_summary: Extract NO3 using chemical method with Cd and sodium azide.

steps.description: The produced N2O was analysed using a pre-concentrator online with an IRMS.
A sub-sample of lyophilized, centrifuged and filtered soil material served for total dissolved N (TDN) determination using an EA-CF-IRMS system.
inputs: lyophilized, centrifuged, and filtered soil material

steps.description: A mass balance calculation using the proportions and δ15N values of the TDN and DIN helped determine the final δ15N values of the DON.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate mass balance to determine δ15N of DON.

methods.method_name: Preliminary Interpretation of Integrated Data - Fate of Airborne N
methods.description: This method involves interpreting integrated data to understand the fate of airborne nitrogen in different soil conditions, focusing on microbial processes and isotopic trends.

steps.description: The contrasting rates of microbial ammonification and nitrification between more and less acidic soils support this hypothesis.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze microbial ammonification and nitrification rates in soils.
outputs: support for hypothesis on N fate

steps.description: The acidic SV and SC showing the increasing δ15N trends have higher ammonification, but lower nitrification rates than less acidic soils.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare δ15N trends and microbial rates in acidic vs. less acidic soils.

steps.description: The integration of the tree-ring, root, EcM and soil isotopic data along with the soil microbial N-transformation rates, pH and microbiome results indicate distinct responses to anthropogenic N emissions.
steps.searchable_summary: Integrate isotopic and microbial data to assess N emission responses.

steps.description: The strong correlations of tree-ring δ15N trends with anthropogenic N emissions imply that the tree-ring series have the potential to record past changes in soil N availability.
steps.searchable_summary: Correlate tree-ring δ15N trends with anthropogenic N emissions.
outputs: potential for recording past soil N changes
